findings.description: EKS cluster 'govcloud-k8s-prod' Kubernetes API server endpoint has public access enabled. The Kubernetes API should be accessible only from within the GovCloud VPC for a FedRAMP High system. Public exposure of the API endpoint violates NIST 800-53 SC-7 (Boundary Protection) and enables direct brute-force attacks against cluster credentials.
findings.remediation: Disable public endpoint access on EKS cluster. Enable private endpoint access. Access the API only via VPN or Direct Connect from authorized government networks. Update kubectl configuration to use private endpoint via AWS VPN.

findings.description: KMS key 'govcloud-data-key' used for encrypting CUI documents in S3 and PostgreSQL does not have automatic key rotation enabled. NIST 800-53 SC-12(1) requires cryptographic key rotation. The key has not been rotated since system deployment 18 months ago.
findings.remediation: Enable automatic key rotation (1-year cycle) for all KMS CMKs. For keys older than 1 year, perform manual key rotation immediately. Document key custodians and rotation schedule in the System Security Plan (SSP).

findings.description: IAM Role 'govcloud-developer-role' used by CI/CD pipeline has an inline policy with Action='*' and Resource='arn:aws-us-gov:s3:::govcloud-cui-*'. Wildcard actions grant all S3 operations (including s3:DeleteObject and s3:PutBucketPolicy) on CUI document buckets. Violates NIST 800-53 AC-6 (Least Privilege).
findings.remediation: Replace wildcard Action with specific required actions: s3:GetObject, s3:PutObject, s3:ListBucket. Remove s3:DeleteObject and s3:PutBucketPolicy from the CI/CD role. Apply IAM Permission Boundaries to all developer roles.

findings.description: CloudTrail trail 'govcloud-audit-trail' does not have log file validation enabled. FedRAMP High requires tamper-evident audit logs (NIST 800-53 AU-9). Without log file validation, audit logs can be modified or deleted without detection — rendering the audit trail inadmissible for incident response or compliance audit.
findings.remediation: Enable CloudTrail log file validation. Configure S3 Object Lock on the CloudTrail bucket with GOVERNANCE mode and 7-year retention. Enable CloudWatch alarms for CloudTrail configuration changes (CIS control 3.5).

findings.description: RDS PostgreSQL 16 instance 'govcloud-postgresql-prod' is not using the FIPS-validated endpoint (rds.us-gov-east-1.amazonaws.com uses FIPS 140-2 endpoint via fips.rds.us-gov-east-1.amazonaws.com). The application connection string uses the standard endpoint. FIPS 140-2 validated cryptography is required for FedRAMP High processing of CUI data.
findings.remediation: Update all database connection strings to use the FIPS endpoint: fips.rds.us-gov-east-1.amazonaws.com. Verify TLS cipher suites are restricted to FIPS-approved algorithms (AES-128-GCM, AES-256-GCM). Update application configuration and rotate credentials after endpoint migration.

findings.description: GuardDuty is enabled in the GovCloud region with findings exported to Security Lake. Threat intelligence feeds include GovCloud-specific threat data. Malware Protection for S3 is enabled on CUI document buckets. Alerts forwarded to the government SOC within the required 1-hour notification SLA.

AWS Security Hub is enabled but the NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5 standard is not enabled. Only the AWS Foundational Security Best Practices standard is active. FedRAMP High requires continuous monitoring against NIST 800-53 controls (CA-7). Without the NIST standard, compliance posture against FedRAMP controls is not automatically tracked.
findings.remediation: Enable NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5 standard in Security Hub. Review current control failures against the NIST standard. Integrate Security Hub findings with the Continuous Monitoring strategy documented in the SSP.

findings.description: GovCloud VPC 'govcloud-main-vpc' does not have VPC Flow Logs enabled. All network traffic to/from government classified workloads must be logged for audit (NIST 800-53 AU-12). Without flow logs, network-based intrusion attempts, data exfiltration, and lateral movement cannot be detected through network forensics.
findings.remediation: Enable VPC Flow Logs for all traffic (accepted and rejected) to CloudWatch Logs. Set 2-year retention. Forward to Security Lake for SIEM correlation. Enable enhanced flow logs (Traffic Mirroring) for high-sensitivity subnets.

findings.description: S3 bucket 'govcloud-cui-documents-prod' storing Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI) documents does not have S3 Object Lock enabled. Without Object Lock, documents can be deleted or overwritten — violating records retention requirements under NIST 800-53 AU-11 and government record-keeping regulations.
findings.remediation: Enable S3 Object Lock in COMPLIANCE mode with 3-year default retention. Enable versioning. Configure S3 Intelligent-Tiering for cost-effective long-term retention. Ensure deletion events are logged and require MFA-delete for any overrides.

findings.description: AWS Config is enabled in us-gov-east-1 but not in us-gov-west-1 (DR region). The disaster recovery environment is not monitored for configuration drift. A misconfiguration introduced during failover testing in the DR region would not be detected until manual review. FedRAMP requires consistent configuration management (CM-6) across all authorization boundary regions.
findings.remediation: Enable AWS Config in us-gov-west-1 with the same conformance packs as the primary region. Set up aggregator account to centralize compliance view across both GovCloud regions. Configure cross-region Config Rules for compliance drift detection.
